name: notion
context: Notion workspace automation — pages, databases, blocks, and integrations via the Notion API
info: The Notion ecosystem covers programmatic interaction with Notion workspaces through the official REST API. Agents can create and update pages, query and manage databases with complex filters and sorts, manipulate block-level content, and build both internal integrations and public OAuth apps. The primary SDK is @notionhq/client for JavaScript/TypeScript. All API calls require an integration token obtained from the Notion integrations dashboard. This tree is organized around the jobs an agent actually performs: managing pages, querying databases, composing rich block content, and setting up integrations.
body:
# Notion Ecosystem

Notion is an all-in-one workspace for notes, documents, databases, and project management. Through the Notion API, agents can programmatically create and update pages, query databases with rich filters, manipulate block-level content, and build integrations that connect Notion to external systems.

## Agent Jobs

The primary tasks an agent performs in this ecosystem:

- **Create/update pages** — build new pages under a parent, set properties, append content blocks. See [[notion/pages]].
- **Query databases** — filter and sort database entries, paginate through large result sets, create new databases. See [[notion/databases]].
- **Manipulate blocks** — append, update, and delete block-level content including paragraphs, headings, lists, toggles, callouts, and code blocks. See [[notion/blocks]].
- **Build integrations** — create internal integrations for private use or public OAuth apps for distribution. See [[notion/integrations]].

## API Overview

The Notion API is a REST API that uses JSON request and response bodies. All endpoints are under `https://api.notion.com/v1/`. Authentication uses Bearer tokens issued per integration. The API versioned via the `Notion-Version` header (current: `2022-06-28`).

| Endpoint Group | Base Path | Purpose |
|---------------|-----------|----------|
| Pages | `/v1/pages` | Create, retrieve, update pages |
| Databases | `/v1/databases` | Create, query, retrieve databases |
| Blocks | `/v1/blocks` | Retrieve, update, delete, append children |
| Users | `/v1/users` | List workspace members |
| Search | `/v1/search` | Search across pages and databases |
| Comments | `/v1/comments` | Create and list comments |

## Authentication

Internal integrations use a fixed token starting with `ntn_` (formerly `secret_`). Public integrations use OAuth 2.0 to obtain tokens on behalf of users. All requests must include:

```
Authorization: Bearer <NOTION_TOKEN>
Notion-Version: 2022-06-28
Content-Type: application/json
```

## Rate Limits

The Notion API enforces rate limits per integration:

| Tier | Requests per second | Burst |
|------|-------------------|-------|
| Standard | 3 req/s | 10 |
| Enterprise | Higher limits | Contact sales |

When rate-limited, the API returns HTTP 429 with a `Retry-After` header. Implement exponential backoff with jitter. Batch operations where possible to stay within limits.

## Libraries

- **@notionhq/client** — official JavaScript/TypeScript SDK
- **notion-sdk-py** — community Python SDK
- **go-notion** — community Go client

sections.title: API overview
sections.body: The Notion REST API lives at `https://api.notion.com/v1/`. All requests require the `Notion-Version` header (current: `2022-06-28`) and a Bearer token. Core endpoint groups: Pages (`/v1/pages`), Databases (`/v1/databases`), Blocks (`/v1/blocks`), Users (`/v1/users`), Search (`/v1/search`), and Comments (`/v1/comments`). Responses are JSON. Pagination uses cursor-based `start_cursor` and `page_size` parameters. See [[notion/databases#pagination]] for query pagination patterns.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.body: Internal integrations use a fixed Bearer token (`ntn_...`) from the integrations dashboard. Public integrations use OAuth 2.0: redirect users to Notion's authorization URL, exchange the code for an access token, then use it for API calls. All requests must include `Authorization: Bearer <token>`, `Notion-Version: 2022-06-28`, and `Content-Type: application/json`. See [[notion/integrations#internal-setup]] for internal setup and [[notion/integrations#oauth-flow]] for OAuth configuration.

sections.title: Rate limits
sections.body: Standard integrations are limited to 3 requests per second with a burst of 10. Enterprise workspaces may have higher limits. When rate-limited, the API returns HTTP 429 with a `Retry-After` header. Implement exponential backoff with jitter. Batch block appends and database queries where possible. Cache responses when data freshness permits. See [[notion/databases#pagination]] for efficient pagination to minimize request count.
